L1.area: XHTML output rendering and attribute processing layer

L2.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in the XHTML rendering engine's attribute sanitization logic. When user-controlled attributes from wiki syntax are translated to HTML output, invalid characters in attribute names are not stripped before constructing data attributes, allowing injection of arbitrary HTML attributes including event handlers. Exploitation can lead to arbitrary script execution in the context of another user's session, potentially including server-side code execution if the victim has elevated privileges.

L3.area: XHTML output printer and attribute sanitization within the XML rendering subsystem
L3.description: A cross-site scripting vulnerability exists in the attribute sanitization logic of the XHTML rendering pipeline. When disallowed attributes from XWiki link syntax are translated to prefixed data attributes for XHTML output, invalid characters in the original attribute names are not stripped. This allows crafted attribute names containing characters like forward slashes to inject arbitrary HTML attributes, including JavaScript event handlers. Exploitation leads to script execution in the victim's session context, and can escalate to server-side code execution if the victim has programming rights.

ground_truth.reason: The XHTML attribute sanitization logic prefixes disallowed attribute names with a data attribute prefix but fails to strip invalid characters, allowing injection of arbitrary HTML attributes including JavaScript event handlers via crafted wiki syntax.
